evals.prompt: I want to add SparkScan to my app. Here's my main view controller: EmptyViewController.swift. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes for a retail app.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, writes complete SparkScan integration code into EmptyViewController.swift, and shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown (mentions SPM package and NSCameraUsageDescription)
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present (v7 API used, not deprecated constructor)
License key placeholder (e.g. YOUR_LICENSE_KEY or similar) is present
No symbologies except ean13UPCA and code128 are enabled
DispatchQueue.main.async is used inside the didScanIn callback

evals.prompt: Add SparkScan barcode scanning to ProductListViewController.swift. When a barcode is scanned, add it to the scannedProducts array and reload the table view.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds SparkScan to ProductListViewController alongside the existing table view code. All existing IBOutlets, properties, and methods are preserved.

assertions.text: DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present (v7 API, not deprecated constructor)
DispatchQueue.main.async is used inside the didScanIn callback

evals.prompt: When a barcode is scanned in my app, I want to reject it if the data is '123456789' and show an error message 'Wrong barcode' for 30 seconds before resuming scanning. All other barcodes should show success feedback.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds SparkScanFeedbackDelegate conformance, implements feedback(for:) to return SparkScanBarcodeErrorFeedback for rejected barcodes and SparkScanBarcodeSuccessFeedback for valid ones, and assigns feedbackDelegate on sparkScanView.

assertions.text: resumeCapturingDelay: 30 is set on the error feedback
SparkScanBarcodeSuccessFeedback() is returned for non-rejected barcodes
The rejection condition checks barcode.data == "123456789" or equivalent
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, sparkScanView, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: My app is used in a warehouse where workers wear gloves. Enable hardware trigger button support so they can scan using physical device buttons.
evals.expected_output: The skill enables hardwareTriggerEnabled on SparkScanViewSettings and passes the settings to SparkScanView.

assertions.text: isHardwareTriggerEnabled = true is set on SparkScanViewSettings
SparkScanViewSettings instance is created and configured before being passed to SparkScanView
SparkScanView is initialized with the configured SparkScanViewSettings (not a fresh SparkScanViewSettings())
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to hide the standard SparkScan trigger button and instead use a custom button in my app's UI. When the custom button is tapped, scanning should start. When tapped again while scanning, it should pause.
evals.expected_output: The skill hides the standard trigger button (isTriggerButtonVisible = false), creates a custom UIButton, and implements button actions that call sparkScanView.startScanning() and sparkScanView.pauseScanning().

assertions.text: Button addTarget is used to connect to a method (button.addTarget(...))
sparkScanView.startScanning() is called in the button action method
sparkScanView.pauseScanning() is called for pause functionality
The button is added to the view hierarchy or set up for user interaction
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, sparkScanView, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need a custom trigger button that changes text and behavior based on the scanner state. Show 'START SCANNING' in IDLE state and 'STOP SCANNING' in ACTIVE state.
evals.expected_output: The skill implements SparkScanViewUIDelegate, tracks view state changes, and updates the button text and behavior based on IDLE, ACTIVE, INACTIVE, and ERROR states.

assertions.text: State comparison for .idle or .initial state is present
State comparison for .active state is present
Button text is set to 'START SCANNING' for idle/inactive states
Button text is set to 'STOP SCANNING' for active state
The button action logic switches behavior based on current state
sparkScanView.startScanning() is called for idle/inactive states
sparkScanView.pauseScanning() is called for active state
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, sparkScanView, listener, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: When the trigger button is hidden, I want to let users tap on the view behind the SparkScan preview to pause scanning, just like the standard UI. The gesture should only be enabled when scanning is active.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates a UITapGestureRecognizer, adds it to the view, implements a tap handler that calls sparkScanView.pauseScanning(), and uses SparkScanViewUIDelegate to enable/disable the gesture based on scanner state.

assertions.text: UITapGestureRecognizer is created with target and action
self.view.addGestureRecognizer(...) is called to attach the gesture
A method to handle the tap gesture is implemented
sparkScanView.pauseScanning() is called in the tap handler
tapGestureRecognizer.isEnabled = false when viewState is not active
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, sparkScanView, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to set up SparkScan with an 'Aim-to-Scan' workflow for precise barcode scanning in crowded environments. The preview should always be visible (even when not scanning), target mode should always be enabled, and single scan behavior should be used.
evals.expected_output: The skill configures SparkScanViewSettings with persistent preview behavior and target mode scanning, then passes these settings to SparkScanView.

assertions.text: SDCSparkScanScanningModeTarget is used for the scanning mode
The configured SparkScanViewSettings is passed to SparkScanView initialization
The preview behavior ensures the camera view is always visible
Target mode is enabled for aiming at barcodes
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: I'm building a high-volume warehouse scanning app where we need rapid, continuous barcode scanning without pausing between items. Set up SparkScan with CONTINUOUS scanning behavior and PERSISTENT preview so the camera stays visible the whole time.
evals.expected_output: The skill configures SparkScanViewSettings with continuous scanning behavior and persistent preview behavior (SparkScanPreviewBehavior.persistent), keeping the scanner active and camera always visible.

assertions.text: SparkScanScanningMode wrapper is used (e.g., SparkScanScanningModeDefault or similar)
The configured SparkScanViewSettings is passed to SparkScanView initialization
Continuous behavior allows multiple scans without pausing between items
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, sparkScanView, listener, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to minimize the UI footprint of SparkScan in my app. Configure it so the camera preview only appears when actively scanning, not when idle. All other times it should be hidden.
evals.expected_output: The skill configures SparkScanViewSettings with default preview behavior (SparkScanPreviewBehavior.default), making the preview appear on-demand only during active scanning.

assertions.text: SparkScanScanningMode wrapper is used with the preview behavior
The configured SparkScanViewSettings is passed to SparkScanView initialization
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, sparkScanView, listener, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need a high-volume warehouse app with minimal UI footprint. Set up SparkScan with CONTINUOUS scanning behavior and POPUP preview behavior, so scanning happens rapidly and the preview only shows when needed.
evals.expected_output: The skill configures SparkScanViewSettings with continuous scanning behavior combined with default preview behavior (SparkScanPreviewBehavior.default).

assertions.text: SparkScanScanningMode wrapper combines both continuous behavior and default preview
The configured SparkScanViewSettings is passed to SparkScanView initialization
Scanning is continuous without pausing between items
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, sparkScanView, listener, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: I have a basic empty SwiftUI view and need to integrate SparkScan in it.
evals.expected_output: The skill integrates SparkScan into a SwiftUI View, including DataCaptureContext setup, SparkScan configuration, SparkScanViewContainer for UIViewRepresentable bridging, and SwiftUI state management for scanned barcodes.

assertions.text: SparkScan(settings: is present with symbologies configured
UIViewRepresentable or equivalent SwiftUI bridge for SparkScanView is used
SparkScanListener conformance is present for handling scan results
didScanIn callback is implemented with proper threading
DispatchQueue.main.async is used for UI updates from scan callbacks
prepareScanning() and stopScanning() lifecycle methods are called
.withSparkScan(sparkScanViewController) modifier is applied to the view

evals.prompt: I need to add SparkScan to my SwiftUI app with custom feedback when barcodes are rejected. Reject barcodes with data '999' and show success for valid ones, all handled through SwiftUI state management.
evals.expected_output: The skill integrates SparkScanFeedbackDelegate into a SwiftUI-based SparkScan setup, managing feedback and implementing proper rejection logic.

assertions.text: SwiftUI View structure with SparkScan integration is present
SparkScanBarcodeErrorFeedback is returned for rejected barcodes (data == '999')
SparkScanBarcodeSuccessFeedback is returned for valid barcodes
resumeCapturingDelay is configured on error feedback
